name: Mola de ColldeJou - Circular route through the Sierra de Llaberia
name_original: Mola de ColldeJou - Vuelta circular por la Sierra de Llaberia
region_intro: Jogging around Tivissa offers an opportunity to explore the diverse and rugged natural landscape of the Ribera d'Ebre region in Catalonia, Spain. The area is characterized by the Muntanyes de Tivissa-Vandellòs Protected Natural Area, featuring karstic mountain ranges with rugged relief, limestone rocks, and dolomites. Runners can expect authentic rocky and mountainous Mediterranean scenery, with some routes providing panoramic views. The terrain includes springs, wetlands, natural ponds, and plateaus, offering varied conditions for running. Best jogging routes around Tivissa The most popular jogging route is Mola de ColldeJou - Circular route through the Sierra de Llaberia, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete, traversing the challenging Sierra de Llaberia. Another top favourite among local runners is El Gegantó – la Tossa loop from Tivissa, a difficult 4.5 miles (7.2 km) path that offers significant elevation gain and views over the mountainous landscape. Local runners also love the Bassa de bombers – Coll del Ventall loop from Tivissa, a 12.2 miles (19.7 km) trail leading through the Coll del Ventall area, often completed in about 3 hours. Jogging around Tivissa is defined by karstic mountain ranges, rugged Mediterranean scenery, and varied plateaus. The network of routes offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Tivissa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 950 runners have used komoot to explore Tivissa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Tivissa?
A: Tivissa offers a good selection of running routes, with over 15 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to various fitness levels, though many are rated as difficult due to the region's mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Tivissa's jogging trails?
A: Jogging in Tivissa means encountering a diverse and rugged natural landscape. The trails often traverse the &lt;strong&gt;Muntanyes de Tivissa-Vandellòs Protected Natural Area&lt;/strong&gt;, characterized by karstic mountain ranges, limestone rocks, and dolomites. You can expect authentic rocky and mountainous Mediterranean scenery, with varied terrain including springs, wetlands, natural ponds, and plateaus. Some routes offer significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Tivissa?
A: While many routes in Tivissa are rated as difficult, the region does offer paths that might be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous experience. It's best to check individual route details for elevation gain and surface type. For example, some sections of trails around the natural ponds or plateaus might be more accessible. Always review the route profile before heading out with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Tivissa?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in natural areas like Tivissa, especially within the &lt;strong&gt;Muntanyes de Tivissa-Vandellòs Protected Natural Area&lt;/strong&gt;. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or where wildlife is present, and to check local regulations for specific routes or seasons. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the rugged and sometimes challenging terrain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I might see while running in Tivissa?
A: Tivissa's running routes are rich with natural beauty and historical sites. You might encounter ancient Iberian settlements, abandoned villages, and old watchtowers. The &lt;strong&gt;Sant Blai hermitage&lt;/strong&gt; on the Ventall mountain pass is a significant monument. Natural features include springs, wetlands, and natural ponds. For specific natural highlights, consider exploring areas with gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/224629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de la Dòvia&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canaleta del Bondia&lt;/a&gt;, or lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/345677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riudecanyes Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Tivissa?
A: Yes, Tivissa offers several excellent circular running routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950275565/mola-de-colldejou-circular-route-through-the-sierra-de-llaberia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mola de ColldeJou - Circular route through the Sierra de Llaberia&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 7-mile option. Another challenging loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10421836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Gegantó – la Tossa loop from Tivissa&lt;/a&gt;, which provides significant elevation gain and panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Tivissa?
A: The diverse landscape of Tivissa can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to run, but always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails in Tivissa?
A: Parking availability varies depending on the specific trailhead. Many routes start near the town of Tivissa, where public parking may be available. For trails deeper within the &lt;strong&gt;Muntanyes de Tivissa-Vandellòs Protected Natural Area&lt;/strong&gt;, look for designated parking areas or small pull-offs near trailheads. It's always a good idea to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for specific parking recommendations.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Tivissa?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running experience in Tivissa, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the authentic rocky and mountainous Mediterranean scenery, the challenging nature of the trails, and the panoramic views that stretch as far as the Costa Daurada. The variety of terrain, from karstic mountains to plateaus, also contributes to a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls or gorges?
A: Yes, the Tivissa region is home to several beautiful gorges and even a waterfall. You can explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/224629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de la Dòvia&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canaleta del Bondia&lt;/a&gt; for impressive gorge scenery. For a specific waterfall, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4391952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de la Sabinosa&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural feature that can be incorporated into longer running or hiking routes.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. Tivissa is known for its challenging terrain, making it ideal for experienced runners. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23764452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassa de bombers – Coll del Ventall loop from Tivissa&lt;/a&gt;, spanning nearly 12.2 miles (19.7 km) with significant elevation gain, offer a demanding and rewarding experience. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23764755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassa de bombers – la Tossa loop from Tivissa&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 12.4 miles (20 km) and also features considerable climbs.

Q: Are there any routes with views of the Costa Daurada?
A: Yes, many of the higher elevation running routes in Tivissa, particularly those within the &lt;strong&gt;Muntanyes de Tivissa-Vandellòs Protected Natural Area&lt;/strong&gt;, offer panoramic views. On clear days, these vistas can stretch all the way to the Costa Daurada, providing stunning coastal backdrops to the rugged inland scenery. Look for routes that ascend to higher points or viewpoints for the best chances of these expansive views.
tips: Mont-Redon is a mountain located near Pratdip, in the Sierra de Llaberia, Catalonia. It's a popular destination for hiking and mountaineering enthusiasts due to its stunning views and challenging routes.
La Miranda is a cliff that is more than nine hundred meters high and offers privileged views of the town of Colldejou. There is also the meteorological station of Tivissa-Llaberia.
It is an area known for its natural beauty and is ideal for outdoor activities such as hiking.
